column4:
Memory Trick

“Control” is the clue: TCP manages connection state and delivery behavior.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.6; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze TCP Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column3: UDP is connectionless and does not provide TCP-style recovery or ordering.
column4:
Exam Trap

Do not describe UDP as reliable delivery. Reliability decisions are left outside the UDP transport behavior.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.6; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze UDP Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column2: TCP and UDP operate at OSI Layer {{c1::4}}, the {{c2::transport}} layer.
column3: In this objective, TCP and UDP are transported inside IP.
column4:
Why

Layer 4 is where the transport protocol choice affects reliability, ordering, flow control, and ports.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.6; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze TCP UDP OSI Source::Messer-v170

column2: Ports {{c1::0 through 1023}} are commonly used as non-ephemeral service ports.
column3: Client systems commonly use temporary ephemeral ports for individual sessions.
column4:
Exam Trap

A service port number does not make a service secure. It identifies where the traffic should go.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.6; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze Ports Sockets Source::Messer-v170

column2: FTP active data uses {{c1::TCP 20}}, and FTP control uses {{c2::TCP 21}}.
column3: FTP transfers files between systems and supports operations such as listing, adding, and deleting files.
column4:
Why

This card keeps the source's active-data/control distinction instead of treating FTP as a single-port fact.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.7; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze FTP Ports Source::Messer-v170

column4:
Exam Trap

Keep SSH separate from Telnet: SSH is the encrypted choice, while Telnet is in clear text.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.7; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze HighYield SSH Ports Source::Messer-v170

column3: Telnet provides remote terminal access but sends communication in clear text.
column4:
Exam Trap

If the scenario requires encrypted administration, choose SSH instead of Telnet.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.7; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze Telnet Ports Source::Messer-v170

column3: SMTP is also used when a device sends mail to a mail server.
column4:
Why

This card uses the source context for TCP 25 instead of expanding into alternate mail-submission ports.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.7; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze SMTP Email Ports Source::Messer-v170

column4:
Exam Trap

For firewall questions, remember both the protocol and the port. The source association here is UDP 53.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.7; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze HighYield DNS Ports Source::Messer-v170

column3: DHCP automatically provides IP configuration details such as an IP address and subnet mask.
column4:
Why

DHCP is a foundational automatic-configuration service, so its port association is high-value recall.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.7; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze HighYield DHCP Ports Source::Messer-v170

column3: POP3 is used to receive email from an email server.
column4:
Related Cards

2.1-C012 covers IMAP4, which also receives email but includes mailbox management from multiple clients.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.7; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze POP3 Email Ports Source::Messer-v170

column3: IMAP4 supports email inbox management from multiple clients.
column4:
Exam Trap

SMTP sends or transfers mail; POP3 and IMAP4 receive mail.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.7; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze IMAP4 Email Ports Source::Messer-v170

column3: SMB is used for Windows file sharing and printer sharing.
column4:
Exam Trap

The approved source also lists NetBIOS-related SMB paths, but direct SMB over TCP is the TCP 445 association.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.7; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze SMB Ports Source::Messer-v170

column3: LDAP stores and retrieves information in a network directory.
column4:
Source Note

This card only tests the LDAP TCP 389 association validated in the approved source page.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.7; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze LDAP Ports Source::Messer-v170

column4:
Real Help Desk

RDP is useful but sensitive. Confirm authorization and network exposure before enabling remote desktop access.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.7; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.1
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze HighYield RDP Ports Source::Messer-v170

column3: HTTP is the standard unencrypted web protocol association for Objective 2.1 common-port recall.
column4:
Why

HTTP TCP 80 is a common firewall and service-identification target. Keep it separate from HTTPS TCP 443.
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze HighYield HTTP Ports Source::Messer-v170

column3: HTTPS is the standard encrypted web protocol association for Objective 2.1 common-port recall.
column4:
Exam Trap

Do not treat HTTP and HTTPS as the same port. HTTPS uses TCP 443 in the common-port list.
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze HighYield HTTPS Ports Source::Messer-v170

column3: SNMP is used for network monitoring and management.
column4:
Exam Trap

SNMP is not normal web, file-sharing, or email traffic. It is associated with monitoring and managing network devices.
column9: A+::220-1201::2.1 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::IPAddressingandCommonPorts Cloze HighYield SNMP Ports Source::Messer-v170
